doc.title: Warranties — assurance vs service
doc.event: Tio Co. sells equipment for $80,000. Prepare the required entries for the warranty that accompanies the sale.

variants.label: Standard quality warranty — accrue the expected cost

lines.label: sale price
lines.why: One PO: the equipment. The quality promise isn't a separate sale.
lines.trap: Assurance warranties never touch revenue — they're a COST of the sale.

lines.label: full price, one obligation
lines.why: Nothing deferred; the warranty is bundled quality, not a purchasable service.
lines.trap: Deferring revenue for an assurance warranty is the flip-side error of expensing a service one.

entries.caption: Same period — accrue the expected warranty cost (matching)

lines.label: estimated repairs
lines.why: Matching: the cost of honoring this sale's warranty belongs to this sale's period, estimated now.
lines.trap: Expense when SOLD, not when the repair truck rolls — waiting for actual claims mismatches the periods.

lines.label: the standing obligation
lines.why: Actual repairs later debit this liability (Dr Warranty Liability / Cr Cash, Parts, Wages).
lines.trap: Actual repair costs never hit Warranty Expense a second time.

variants.label: Extended warranty sold for 6,000 — a separate performance obligation

lines.label: both pieces
lines.trap: The extended warranty is something the customer CHOSE to buy — the definition of a separate PO.

lines.label: the equipment PO
lines.why: Recognized at delivery, point in time.
lines.trap: If the two weren't separately priced, you'd ALLOCATE by relative standalone prices first — the ratio machine again.

lines.label: the service PO, deferred
lines.why: A contract liability until the coverage period runs.
lines.trap: Deferred REVENUE, not warranty expense — the service warranty is a product you sold, not a cost you owe.

entries.caption: Each coverage year (3-year term, starting after the assurance period)

lines.why: Released ratably (straight-line default) over the coverage years.
lines.trap: The clock starts when COVERAGE starts — often after the free assurance year ends, not at the sale date.

lines.label: earned by standing ready
lines.trap: Costs of servicing THIS warranty expense as incurred — no accrual-at-sale like assurance.

memorize.body: Assurance warranty → Warranty Expense + Warranty Liability at sale (matching). Service warranty → separate PO: Unearned Warranty Revenue, released over the coverage period.
The tell: could the customer buy it separately (or decline it)? Yes → service (revenue world). No, it just came with the product → assurance (expense world).

questions.prompt: A customer pays 6,000 extra for a 3-year extended warranty. At the sale date this 6,000 is credited to…

distractors.feedback: Not expense-side at all — a PURCHASED warranty is a separate performance obligation: credit Unearned Warranty Revenue 6,000, release 2,000 per coverage year. (Expense-accrual is the ASSURANCE pattern.)

build.scaffold: Classify before journalizing: did they BUY the protection? Service → defer revenue, earn over coverage. Came free with quality? Assurance → full revenue now, accrue the expected repair cost.
